implementation_scope: Develop and integrate user authentication functionality, including Google sign-in and email logins via Firebase Authentication.
Implement user registration process to collect email, full name, role, and company name (required). Do not collect address or telephone.
Build a functional project dashboard displaying active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
Implement thorough testing of the authentication flow.
Configure PostgreSQL for session storage for V1.

constraints.text: Deliver a working Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by September 1st (hard deadline due to marketing campaign launch on September 5th).
The system performance must be very fast.
Backend development must leverage existing Python libraries within the team.
Avoid building a custom authentication system; prefer third-party services.
The PostgreSQL database must be placed within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC) and not exposed to the public internet (strict technical security constraint).
Cloud Run must connect to the database using Serverless VPC Access.
Firebase configuration keys are required by July 8th.

architectural_decisions.text: Database: PostgreSQL, adhering to a standard relational model.
Session management: Standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 (Redis deferred to V2).
User authentication service: Firebase Authentication for V1.
Backend deployment: Cloud Run, with security mitigation via private VPC and Serverless VPC Access for database connectivity.
User session token expiration: Initially set to 24 hours.

open_technical_questions: What are the specific data sources, display components, interactions, and any other metrics/features (beyond active users, system status, and project lists) required for the V1 project dashboard? (Further detailing was deferred, D042)

acceptance_criteria: Given a user is on the registration page, When they fill in a valid Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name, and submit, Then their account is successfully created, they are logged in, and redirected to the project dashboard.

Given a user is on the registration page, When they attempt to register without providing an Email, Then an error message is displayed indicating the Email field is required, and registration is prevented.

Given a user is on the registration page, When they attempt to register without providing a Full Name, Then an error message is displayed indicating the Full Name field is required, and registration is prevented.

Given a user is on the registration page, When they attempt to register without providing a Role, Then an error message is displayed indicating the Role field is required, and registration is prevented.

Given a user is on the registration page, When they attempt to register without providing a Company Name, Then an error message is displayed indicating the Company Name field is required, and registration is prevented.
Given a user is on the login page, When they provide valid email/password credentials, Then they are successfully logged in and redirected to the project dashboard.
Given a user is on the login page, When they click the "Sign in with Google" option, Then they are successfully authenticated via Google and redirected to the project dashboard.

Given a user is logged in, When 24 hours pass since their last successful authentication, Then their session token expires, and subsequent attempts to access protected resources should require re-authentication.
Given a user is on the registration page, When they attempt to provide an address or telephone number, Then these fields are not collected or stored by the system.
Given a user is logged in and navigates to the project dashboard, Then the dashboard displays the active user metrics.
Given a user is logged in and navigates to the project dashboard, Then the dashboard displays the current system status.
Given a user is logged in and navigates to the project dashboard, Then the dashboard displays lists of projects.
Given the backend services are running, Then user session information is stored in the PostgreSQL database.
test_scenarios: **User Registration - Happy Path:** Successfully register a new user using email/password with valid Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name.
**User Registration - Google Sign-in Happy Path:** Successfully register and log in a new user using Google sign-in.
**User Login - Happy Path:** Log in successfully with valid email/password credentials.
**User Login - Google Sign-in Happy Path:** Log in successfully with Google account credentials.
**Dashboard Access - Valid Session:** Log in and verify the project dashboard loads correctly, displaying active user metrics, system status, and project lists.
**User Registration - Invalid Email:** Attempt to register with an incorrectly formatted email address.
**User Registration - Existing Email:** Attempt to register with an email address already associated with an account.
**User Registration - Missing Required Fields:** Attempt to register by omitting Email, Full Name, Role, or Company Name individually and in combination.
**User Login - Incorrect Credentials:** Attempt to log in with incorrect email or password.
**User Login - Non-existent Account:** Attempt to log in with an email that is not registered.
**Session Expiration:** Log in, wait for 24 hours (or simulate session expiry), then attempt to access a protected resource and confirm re-authentication is required.
**Unauthorized Access:** Attempt to access authenticated dashboard features without being logged in or with an expired session.
edge_cases: **User Registration - Max Length Inputs:** Test registration with maximum allowed character lengths for Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name fields (if limits are defined).
**User Registration - Special Characters:** Test registration with Full Name, Role, and Company Name fields containing special characters, symbols, and Unicode characters.
**Concurrent Registrations:** Attempt multiple user registrations simultaneously with identical or similar data.
**Concurrent Logins:** Log in to the same user account from multiple devices or browser tabs simultaneously.

**Session Expiration Handling:** While actively performing actions in the application, simulate session expiration and observe the user experience (e.g., auto-logout, error message on next action, graceful redirection).
**Empty Dashboard Data:** What does the dashboard display if there are no active users, no system status information, or no projects?
unresolved_behaviors: The definition of "very fast" system performance (D005) needs concrete, measurable metrics (e.g., specific page load times, API response thresholds, concurrent user capacity).
The precise definition, calculation method, and expected display format for "Active User Metrics" (D039) on the project dashboard are unclear.
The specific components and states included in "System Status" (D040) for display on the dashboard are unclear.
The full scope of data and display format for "Project Lists" (D041) on the dashboard is currently undefined. Further details are deferred to the next sync with marketing (D042, D047).
Are there any specific validation rules (e.g., predefined list, character limits) for the 'Role' field during user registration?
Are there any specific validation rules (e.g., character limits, allowed characters, uniqueness requirements) for the 'Company Name' field during user registration?
What is the expected user experience and system behavior when a user's session expires while they are actively using the application (e.g., notification, immediate logout, silent refresh attempt)?
Are there any specific password complexity requirements (e.g., minimum length, use of special characters, numbers) for email logins via Firebase Authentication?

decision_history: The primary objective for Version 1 (V1) is to deliver a functional Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by September 1st, a hard deadline driven by the marketing campaign launching on September 5th.
Core features for V1 confirmed: User authentication and a functional project dashboard.
A key non-functional requirement is for the system performance to be 'very fast'.
Technology stack decisions: React for the frontend, Python (FastAPI) for the backend (leveraging existing team libraries), and PostgreSQL as the primary database.
For V1, session management will use standard PostgreSQL sessions to prioritize simplicity and budget; Redis is deferred to V2 unless performance becomes a critical blocker.

User authentication for V1 will be integrated via Firebase Authentication, chosen for its rapid implementation, support for social logins, and alignment with pricing requirements. It will support Google sign-in and email logins. The team explicitly decided against building a custom authentication system.

User registration will require the collection of Email, Full Name, Role, and Company Name (the latter being essential due to the product's B2B nature). Address and telephone number are not required for initial signup.
The deployment strategy has been confirmed: Vercel for the React frontend and Cloud Run for the FastAPI backend.

To address Cloud Run security concerns, the PostgreSQL database will be placed in a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC), with Cloud Run connecting via Serverless VPC Access. It is a strict technical constraint that the PostgreSQL instance must not be exposed to the public internet.
Token expiration for user sessions is initially set to 24 hours, with the understanding that this can be adjusted later.

The project dashboard will display active user metrics, system status, and project lists. However, further detailing of these dashboard features is specifically postponed to the next synchronization meeting.
Mike has been assigned the responsibility for Firebase provisioning, with a target to deliver the configuration keys by July 8th.
The authentication flow must undergo thorough testing.
dependencies: The V1 MVP delivery by September 1st is a hard dependency for the marketing campaign launch on September 5th.
Marketing advertisements have already been booked, making the September 1st V1 deadline non-negotiable.
Engineering work on authentication is dependent on Mike delivering Firebase configuration keys by July 8th.
Finalizing detailed dashboard requirements and design is dependent on a scheduled synchronization meeting with the marketing team next Wednesday.
risks: Missed V1 MVP Deadline (High Impact, High Likelihood if unforeseen issues occur): The September 1st deadline is a hard constraint due to the marketing campaign launch on September 5th, with ads already booked. Missing this deadline would have severe business repercussions.

Performance Bottleneck with PostgreSQL Sessions (Medium Impact, Low-Medium Likelihood): The decision to use standard PostgreSQL sessions for V1 to save budget and simplify could become a performance blocker if the 'very fast' system performance requirement is not met, potentially necessitating a costly and time-consuming pivot to Redis later.

Incomplete Dashboard Requirements Leading to Rework (Medium Impact, Medium Likelihood): The deferral of detailed dashboard feature planning to the next sync meeting carries a risk of delays or rework if requirements are not fully captured and agreed upon in a timely manner, especially with the approaching MVP deadline.

Database Security Vulnerability (High Impact, Low Likelihood if mitigation followed): While a comprehensive security mitigation plan for Cloud Run and PostgreSQL deployment is established, any failure in implementing these strict technical constraints (e.g., public exposure of DB, misconfigured VPC access) could lead to a critical security breach.
alignment_gaps: The requirement for 'very fast' system performance lacks specific, quantifiable metrics. This ambiguity could lead to differing interpretations among stakeholders and potential rework if performance expectations are not mutually understood and met.

While the required fields for user registration have been identified, the overarching 'acceptance criteria' for the user registration process are still undefined. This could lead to gaps in user experience or testing if not clarified.
open_questions: What are the specific, quantifiable acceptance criteria for 'very fast' system performance?
What are the detailed acceptance criteria for the user registration process, beyond the confirmed fields (email, full name, role, company name)?
What are the remaining detailed features and specific metrics to be displayed on the project dashboard, further elaborating on 'active user metrics', 'system status', and 'project lists'?
milestones: Deliver a working V1 Minimum Viable Product (MVP) by 2024-09-01.
Firebase configuration keys delivered by Mike by 2024-07-08.

system_requirements: The system SHALL deliver a functional Minimum Viable Product (MVP) for V1.
The system SHALL implement user authentication as a core feature for V1.
The system SHALL implement a functional project dashboard as a core feature for V1.
The system SHALL exhibit very fast performance (inferred — needs confirmation of specific metrics like response times).
The system SHALL utilize PostgreSQL for storing user session information.
The system SHALL integrate with Firebase Authentication for user authentication in V1.
Firebase Authentication SHALL be configured to support Google sign-in.
Firebase Authentication SHALL be configured to support email logins.
The user registration process SHALL collect the user's email address, full name, role, and company name.
The user registration process SHALL NOT collect the user's address or telephone number for V1.
The company name field SHALL be a required input during the user signup process.
The authentication flow SHALL be thoroughly tested.
The project dashboard SHALL display active user metrics.
The project dashboard SHALL display the system status.
The project dashboard SHALL display lists of projects.
data_flows: Frontend (React application) <-> Firebase Authentication: User authentication data (login credentials, tokens, user profiles).
Frontend (React application) <-> Backend (FastAPI service): User session information, project dashboard data (active user metrics, system status, project lists).
Backend (FastAPI service) <-> PostgreSQL Database: User session data, application data (e.g., user profiles, project details, dashboard metrics).
Backend (FastAPI service) -> Logging/Monitoring System (inferred from 'system status' dashboard metric): System operational data and events.
interface_contracts: Firebase Authentication Integration: Standard OAuth 2.0 / OpenID Connect flows for Google sign-in and email/password authentication.
Firebase Authentication Token: Issued JWTs with an expiration of 24 hours.

User Registration API Endpoint (FastAPI): Expected payload includes 'email' (string), 'fullName' (string), 'role' (string), 'companyName' (string). 'companyName' is a required field. (inferred — needs confirmation of specific field names and types).
Project Dashboard API Endpoints (FastAPI): (inferred — details to be confirmed) Endpoints for retrieving active user metrics, system status, and project lists.

constraints.text: The Version 1 (V1) Minimum Viable Product (MVP) MUST be delivered by September 1st.
The V1 MVP delivery is constrained by a marketing campaign launch on September 5th.
The backend development stack selection is influenced by existing Python libraries within the team.
The PostgreSQL database instance MUST be deployed within a private Virtual Private Cloud (VPC).
The Cloud Run backend service MUST connect to the PostgreSQL database using Serverless VPC Access.
The PostgreSQL database instance MUST NOT be exposed to the public internet.
Firebase configuration keys are required by next week (July 8th).

architectural_decisions.text: Backend service development SHALL use Python with FastAPI.
The primary database SHALL be PostgreSQL, adhering to a standard relational model.
User session management for V1 SHALL use standard PostgreSQL sessions, deferring Redis to V2 unless performance issues arise.
User authentication SHALL be handled by integrating Firebase Authentication for V1.
The React frontend application SHALL be deployed using Vercel.
The FastAPI backend application SHALL be deployed using Cloud Run, with the database placed in a private VPC and Cloud Run connecting via Serverless VPC Access for security.
User session token expiration SHALL be set to 24 hours.
Further detailing of project dashboard features is deferred until the next synchronization meeting.
Mike is assigned ownership of Firebase provisioning.

open_analysis_questions: What are the precise definitions, data sources, refresh rates, and display formats for the 'active user metrics', 'system status', and 'project lists' to be shown on the project dashboard? (Follow up on D042)
Blocked by: acceptance criteria for user registration (owned by PM/QA - D019)

stakeholder.business_summary: The team is committed to delivering the first version (MVP) of the product by September 1st, a firm deadline driven by upcoming marketing campaigns. This initial release will focus on core features like user sign-in and a basic project dashboard, designed for fast performance and essential B2B data collection.
key_impact: A firm launch date of September 1st is established, crucial for aligning with the planned marketing campaign starting September 5th.
Users will have a straightforward and secure way to sign in, including via Google, and will gain access to a foundational project dashboard.
Development leverages standard, cost-effective technologies, helping to manage project budget and timeline for the initial release.
The system will collect vital B2B user information, such as company name and role, directly during registration, supporting future business insights and sales efforts.
A secure deployment strategy has been adopted, ensuring sensitive customer data is protected and not exposed publicly.
decisions_needing_alignment: The complete and detailed requirements for the project dashboard features need to be finalized. A sync with the marketing team is scheduled for next Wednesday to discuss the design.
major_unresolved_questions: What specific metrics and functionalities are critical for the project dashboard beyond active users, system status, and project lists?
Is the default 24-hour user session duration appropriate for business users, or should it be extended or shortened based on usage patterns or security policies?

review.reviewer_notes: All role outputs are consistent with the extracted decisions and maintain high quality. There are no hallucinations, contradictions, or missing critical information. Open questions and areas needing further definition are appropriately flagged across the different views. No prompt injection or sensitive content was detected.

evidence_quotes: Let's make sure we sync with the marketing team on the dashboard design next Wednesday.
